skill.title: SafeStore Core Class
skill.description: Primary API for local semantic storage, vectorization, and document management.
skill.content:
# Artifact System Skill – Best Practices (2026 Edition)
**Purpose**: Teach the model how to correctly use persistent **Artifacts** while avoiding common failure modes (especially nesting `<artifact>` inside markdown code blocks and type anchoring).
## Core Concept
Artifacts are **persistent, version-controlled** objects (code files, documents, notes, skills, prompts, etc.). They are **completely separate** from temporary markdown code blocks.
### Critical Distinction (Always Emphasize First)
- **Markdown code blocks** (` ```python ... ``` `) → **Temporary display** only. Use them to show code to the user. - **Artifacts** (`<artifact>...</artifact>` XML tags) → **Persistent storage** with automatic versioning.
**❌ NEVER do this** (this mistake breaks artifact creation):
```xml <artifact name="example.ext" type="type"> ... </artifact> ```
**✅ Correct way**: Output the `<artifact>` tag **directly** as raw XML in the response, never inside any markdown fence.
## Key Prompting Principles Applied (2026 Best Practices)
- **Primacy Effect**: Place the most critical guardrails (distinction + anti-pattern) at the very beginning. - **Recency Effect**: Reinforce the most important rules at the end. - **Avoid Anchoring Bias**: Use neutral examples and explicitly instruct against defaulting to one type (e.g., python). - **Clarity & Structure**: Use clear sections, ❌/✅ pairs, and consistent formatting. - **Output Contract**: Always require a short human-readable explanation outside the XML tag. - **Lost-in-the-Middle Avoidance**: Keep instructions focused and well-delimited.
## Supported Artifact Types (Insert your actual list here, e.g.:) `python, javascript, typescript, html, css, markdown, text, json, yaml, toml, sql, prompt, skill, ...`
**Rule**: Always choose the **most accurate type** for the content. Never default to any single language.
## How to Use Artifacts
You **MUST** always add a short, natural explanation **outside** the XML tag explaining what you did and why. Never respond with only XML tags.
### Option A: CREATE or REPLACE (Full Content)
```xml <artifact name="filename.ext" type="appropriate_type"> Full content of the artifact goes here... </artifact> ```
### Option B: HANDLE (Reference previous code block – no duplication)
Use when you already showed code in a markdown block earlier in the conversation.
```xml <artifact name="filename.ext" type="appropriate_type" handle="msg_idx:block_idx" /> ```
- `msg_idx`: 0-based index of the message - `block_idx`: 0-based index of the fenced code block inside that message
### Option C: PATCH (Targeted Search/Replace Updates)
Best for small, precise changes.
```xml <artifact name="filename.ext" type="appropriate_type"> <<<<<<< SEARCH exact old lines here (copy verbatim) ======= new lines here >>>>>>> REPLACE
<<<<<<< SEARCH another exact section ======= replacement text >>>>>>> REPLACE </artifact> ```
**Patch Rules**: - Keep each SEARCH block short (ideally 1–5 lines) - SEARCH must match **exactly** (character by character) - You can have multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks in one tag - Always end with `>>>>>>> REPLACE`
### Option D: RENAME + UPDATE
```xml <artifact name="new_name.ext" type="appropriate_type" rename="old_name.ext"> ... full content or SEARCH/REPLACE blocks ... </artifact> ```
### Option E: REVERT
```xml <artifact name="filename.ext" revert_to="v3" /> ```
## Final Reminder (Recency Boost)
→ Artifacts = persistent & versioned → Markdown code blocks = temporary display only → Never nest `<artifact>` inside ``` blocks → Always choose the correct `type` — never default → Always add a clear human explanation outside the tag
**End of Artifact System Skill** ```
### How to Use This Skill in the Future
1. Copy the entire content into a file named something like `artifact_system_skill.md`. 2. In your lollms setup, load this as a **skill** or inject relevant sections into the system prompt when artifact functionality is needed. 3. You can also reference specific parts (e.g., "Follow the Critical Distinction and Option C from the Artifact System Skill").
This Markdown version is self-contained, easy to update, and follows current 2026 prompting best practices: strong guardrails upfront, neutral examples, explicit anti-bias rules, and structured sections.
Would you like me to make a shorter "cheat-sheet" version, add a few neutral few-shot examples, or turn this into a ready-to-inject system prompt block?
